contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 180
- Description: Oracle advisor refactor: lens-led zone selection, day-anchored QMDJ, Opus 4.7
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/125
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/126
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/141
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/oracle/pull/14
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/oracle/pull/15
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/oracle/pull/16
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/oracle/pull/17
  
  Details:
  Oracle advisor refactor at oracle.truesight.me — diagnostic + rebuild end-to-end this session:
  
  Diagnosis: the existing advisor was hill-climbing to the retailer sales pipeline regardless of what the I Ching cast was pointing at. Root cause: ADVISORY_SNAPSHOT.md had ~13:1 operational-funnel-to-mission volume ratio; the model followed the input distribution gradient. The prompt's "treat I Ching as a lens" guardrail could not overcome the asymmetry.
  
  Three structural responses:
  
  1. Snapshot rebalance — new "Recent ecosystem activity (Telegram Chat Logs)" section in the generator pulls the last 50 rows of the Telegram & Submissions sheet (contributions, practice events, partner check-ins, inventory moves, currency conversions, sales). Placed BETWEEN Meta and Growth Goals so real-time non-retail signal sits above the funnel. Cron updated to keep the flag through 6h refresh cycles.
  
  2. Prompt restructured for daily-cast cadence + lens-led zone selection. Dropped "priorities this week" and "next 24h" framings — replaced with a single "today's move" register. Model now: (a) reads the cast in its own terms first, (b) surveys candidate zones across the whole ecosystem (lineage / relational / supply / retail / tooling / contemplative pause / outside-the-DAO), (c) picks the zone the hexagram is pointing at justified on the cast's symbolism, (d) names ONE move's shape and scale for today, (e) names only decision-flipping context gaps with a stated counterfactual. Tightened twice in response to live feedback: gaps must be decision-flipping (was producing structural-absence noise); today's move names shape + scale not script content (was inventing topics and rhetorical constraints).
  
  3. Day-anchored QMDJ as default + UI toggle. The qimen-dunjia library's hour pillar locked to noon of the cast date in day mode, so one cast per day produces one chart. Classical hour-based 時家奇門 still available via a radio toggle in the QMDJ panel; choice persisted in localStorage. chart_mode flows to the model so it knows whether it's reading a daily disposition or an hourly window.
  
  Operator-agnostic framing: depersonalized the prompt from "Gary" to "the operator" so the oracle works for any drawer who casts (single-player-RPG model).
  
  Model upgrade: claude-sonnet-4-6 → claude-opus-4-7 by default. Opus 4.7 holds the multiple lens / scale / cadence guardrails better against the snapshot's gravity.
  
  Live verification: oracle.truesight.me deployment bumped to v12. Local instance spun up at http://127.0.0.1:8091/ for operator review. Multiple prompt revisions interleaved with operator feedback shipped same-session.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 300.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
--------
